acceptanceCriteria: scripts/package-plugin.mjs exports buildArchiveTar (the uncompressed, runtime-independent ustar payload) and buildArchive returns it as `tar` while still gzipping the attested `.tar.gz`.

scripts/install-verified.mjs seed-integrity check compares sha256(buildArchiveTar(extracted seed)) against sha256(gunzip(held archive)) instead of a recompressed .tar.gz digest, so a legitimate attested release promotes regardless of the consumer Node/zlib deflate build; content tampering still fails closed.

test/provenance.test.ts adds a #26 regression test proving the tar-layer check is invariant to gzip deflate encoding and still rejects tampered content, plus a buildArchiveTar/tar-exposure test; the recorded git-diff-check passes and `npm run test:all` (373 tests) is rerun and reported in the PR.

.github/workflows/consumer-portability.yml packages the plugin on the pinned producer Node 22 and runs the reference wrapper on different consumer Node runtimes (20 and 24), asserting the wrapper promotes and the extracted-tree tar digest matches; the workflow requests only contents: read.

docs/provenance.md, docs/threat-model.md, and docs/adr/0001-provenance-trust-model.md describe the seed-integrity check as a tar-layer comparison and the consumer copy list includes scripts/package-plugin.mjs.
note: Policy permits transmission; minimize content and re-check destination controls.
